refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the latency of data acquisition and processing separately, then confirm the combined latency meets the p95 ≤ 1.0 s target under supported update scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry links for data acquisition
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency

potential_sources: Data acquisition and processing are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interfaces
Telemetry data format and processing algorithm inputs must be tightly coordinated
coupling_check.notes: The chosen DPs maintain a decoupled architecture with well-defined data flow, minimizing coupling risks.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry network can sustain required bandwidth and latency under operational conditions
Processing algorithms can be optimized without sacrificing accuracy or safety
Risk of latency spikes due to network congestion or processing overload
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry network performance and processing load; consider fallback or adaptive algorithms if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry acquisition and real-time processing to update the worksite model

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from vehicles and sensors

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to other system components or operator interfaces

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE Solution and remote modules including Teleoperation and Safety Services

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as the NGHE Solution integrates both acquisition and processing; low diffusion risk due to centralized update logic.
Limited containment; vehicle provides raw telemetry but does not process updates.
Primarily consumes updated model data; minimal role in update latency.
Safety Services consume alerts but do not participate in model update.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency functions within NGHE Solution minimizes interface complexity and latency diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Risk of single point of failure impacting update latency
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider modular internal partitioning within NGHE Solution to isolate acquisition and processing; implement monitoring and fallback mechanisms.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are clearly addressed by the selected DPs with logical mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized implementation in NGHE Solution
Potential risk if internal complexity grows without modularization
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the best IM boundary for these DPs.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end and at sub-function levels within NGHE Solution.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution IM with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor internal complexity within NGHE Solution to avoid bottlenecks
Plan for fallback or adaptive strategies if latency targets are not consistently met

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the latency requirement with clear rationale and manageable risks. Verification approaches are feasible and interfaces are well-defined.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level if desired.

assumptions: Telemetry network performance meets latency and bandwidth requirements
Processing algorithms can be optimized for real-time performance
open_risks: Network congestion or failures impacting latency
Processing overload or software faults causing delays
evidence_needed: Latency measurements from telemetry network under operational load
Performance benchmarks of processing algorithms in realistic scenarios
